creator: cem@freebsd.org
text: In fact, FreeBSD does something similar, but produces a warning first (ipseclog LOG_DEBUG, "reqid=%d range violation, updated by kernel").  That code is present since 2002.  I can't tell if libcharon is broken on Linux and merely doesn't observe it there, or if it's just poorly designed.  I don't know if pfsense has any modifications to FreeBSD in this area that might be relevant.  Can you reproduce the problem on FreeBSD, or just pfsense?
